text: For a 5-inch-thick outer reflector and a 1-ft-diameter island, both beryllium, the calculated critical mass is $108\mathrm{kg}$ ; 40 percent of the fissions occur at thermal, and the maximum power density of 3.9 times the core mean power density occurs at the island-salt interface.

If the reflector thickness is increased to 10 inches, the critical mass is reduced to $34\mathrm{kg}$ ; 67 percent of the fissions occur at thermal, and the peak power density of twice the core mean again occurs at the core island-salt interface.
